summary: Full stack testing of Stakked app. Fixed critical backend bug (get_current_user returning _id instead of id). All 14 backend tests pass. UI flows tested end-to-end. Core editor functionality working. Drag-and-drop lands elements but canvas element detection uncertain.

critical.issue: FIXED: get_current_user returned user['_id'] but endpoints used user['id'], causing KeyError and 500 on all page operations
critical.fix_applied: Changed user['_id'] = str(user['_id']) to user['id'] = str(user.pop('_id')) in server.py:get_current_user

minor.issue: No /api/gallery endpoint exists - gallery uses /api/pages/published. Frontend should confirm it uses correct endpoint.

ui_bugs.issue: Drag from element tray to canvas - element visually dragged but [data-testid='canvas-el-'] not found after drop via mouse drag. DragEvent dispatch also not confirmed to land element. Needs manual verification.

issues: 3 empty dark skeleton cards show on empty workspace - looks like loading state that persists briefly. Fine as loading UX.

action_items: Verify drag-and-drop works correctly in browser - canvas element may not be registering drop events properly
Consider adding test user cleanup for TEST_ prefix pages created during backend tests
critical_code_review_comments: FIXED: get_current_user bug - user dict had '_id' key but all endpoints expected 'id' - was 500ing all page APIs
No /api/gallery endpoint - frontend gallery page should be using /api/pages/published
brute force lockout uses IP+email key which works correctly
bcrypt hash format verified - uses $2b$ prefix correctly via bcrypt.gensalt()
seed_data_creation: Backend tests created TEST_Page, TEST_GetPage, TEST_Updated, TEST_Publish pages for test@stakked.io. TEST_Publish was published and is visible in gallery.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend bug fixed: get_current_user now returns 'id' not '_id'. All auth and page CRUD APIs work. Editor UI looks excellent with proper 3-col layout. Theme drawer (5 themes), AI dock, publish modal all work. Drag from tray to canvas needs manual verification - automated drag may not trigger React DnD correctly.
rca_of_issue: Root cause: get_current_user function in server.py was setting user['_id'] = str(user['_id']) keeping '_id' key, but all page endpoints used user['id']. This caused KeyError on all /api/pages/* endpoints returning HTTP 500. Fix: use user.pop('_id') to remove old key and set user['id'].
